competition.narrative: The competitive field is holistic and recency-weighted. (1) Globality (incumbent sourcing platform, active): BT currently uses Globality for sourcing ('We use Globality for sourcing', Shane, Avoma, 30 June 2026). Threat level: high (incumbent advantage, existing relationship, data integration). Status: active (in play for replacement via the AI sourcing RFI). How we win: prove Zycus agentic AI sourcing (Anna, autonomous negotiation, bid recommendation) is production-ready and superior to Globality's AI, with named enterprise references. (2) Certio (incumbent savings tracking platform, active): BT uses Certio for savings tracking ('We use Certio for saving, tracking', Shane, Avoma, 30 June 2026). Threat level: medium (incumbent, but Shane expressed dissatisfaction: 'is it the perfect tool?'). Status: active (in play for replacement via the savings management RFI). How we win: prove Zycus savings management with lifecycle tracking and milestone/delta capture (Shane's stated need) exceeds Certio's capability. (3) GEP (RFI participant, active): Anthony Gray stated 'I know you'll probably be inviting people like Zip and GEP… we're winning in that space. We are a real challenger' (Avoma, 30 June 2026), confirming GEP is a known RFI participant and Zycus peer threat. Threat level: high (live RFI finalist, peer competitor in orchestration). Status: active. How we win: position Zycus orchestration + AI sourcing + savings as an integrated best-of-breed suite vs GEP's point-solution gaps, with production references. (4) Zip (RFI participant, active): Anthony stated 'people like Zip' (Avoma, 30 June 2026). Threat level: medium (RFI participant). Status: active. How we win: same as GEP. Summary: Globality and GEP are the strongest current threats (incumbent and peer finalist). Certio and Zip are medium threats. Zycus missed the RFI but can compete if invited into the RFP by proving production-readiness and reference-backed superiority.

identify_pain.narrative: Shane Gallagher articulated three operational pain points on the 30 June 2026 call. (1) Savings tracking lacks lifecycle context: 'historically, that context would be a wash because no one can sit down and go through all that. But actually now with sort of the brute force of AI, we can kind of find insights in that. So that's savings tracking has become one of interest to us' (Avoma, 30 June 2026). Pain owner: Shane and the procurement excellence function (Simen Wefald's team). (2) Orchestration/front-door chaos: BT issued an RFI for 'heavy orchestration, sort of wider than the source to the contract, but also like a replacement for our front door' (Shane, Avoma, 30 June 2026), implying the current intake/orchestration approach is inadequate. Pain owner: procurement operations (source-to-contract and P2P teams). (3) AI sourcing capability gap: BT is evaluating 'an agentic solution' for AI sourcing (Shane, Avoma, 30 June 2026), implying Globality (current sourcing platform) does not meet their agentic/AI sourcing needs. Pain owner: sourcing teams. No quantified impact (cost, time, error rate) was shared for any pain point. All pain is confirmed qualitative; financial/operational impact is inferred but not stated.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting, legal, procurement, InfoSec, or ERP integration process was discussed on the 30 June 2026 call. No evidence of MSA/SOW requirements, legal review, security questionnaire, or systems integration scoping. BT Sourced operates across multiple geographies (Ireland, UK, Hungary, India) and multiple business units (BT Group, consumer, Openreach) with different ERP systems (Shane stated 'consumer and Openreach have different ordering channels/ERPs'), so integration complexity is likely high, but no specifics were explored. No SI/partner involvement mentioned. No current-contract expiration or forcing function. Recommended move: after securing RFP participation, map the paper process by asking Shane (or a procurement/IT stakeholder) about BT's contracting requirements, InfoSec review gates, ERP integration touchpoints, and typical procurement-technology contracting cycle time.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is RFI-driven: BT issued orchestration and AI sourcing RFIs over a month before 30 June 2026, received submissions, and is now evaluating them internally. Shane stated 'submissions are already in. There's an evaluation at the moment' (Avoma, 30 June 2026). An RFP is expected after the RFI evaluation: Shane said 'there will be an RFP' (Avoma, 30 June 2026), but timing is unknown. The process will likely be: RFI evaluation → RFP shortlist → vendor demos → reference calls → commercial negotiation → contract. Shane's role is to review vendor materials, bring them to 'a couple of people' internally, and decide whether to proceed to a live demo (Avoma, 30 June 2026). The decision committee composition is unknown (who evaluates the RFI, who shortlists for the RFP, who approves the budget). No evidence of approval chain, multi-stakeholder review gates, or final decision authority. The call sequence so far: one discovery call (30 June 2026) with Shane, Anthony Gray (Zycus VP Europe), Claire Hudson (Zycus RSD), and Shriya Iyer (Zycus BDR). No escalation, no senior stakeholders, no executive engagement. Recommended move: ask Shane to map the RFI evaluation committee and the RFP decision process (who evaluates, who scores, who shortlists, who owns the final decision) and to introduce the deal owner to at least one decision committee member.

decision_criteria.narrative: BT Sourced ran an orchestration RFI and an AI sourcing RFI (both closed over a month before 30 June 2026), so the evaluation criteria are documented in those RFI questions, but Zycus does not have access to them. Shane stated the evaluation is underway: 'submissions are already in. There's an evaluation at the moment' (Avoma, 30 June 2026). The confirmed criteria from the discovery call include: (1) orchestration capability (guided front door, wider than source-to-contract, replacement for existing front door), (2) AI sourcing (agentic solution), (3) savings management with lifecycle tracking and milestone/delta capture, (4) point-solution approach (integration with existing tools, not a full suite replacement), (5) production-ready deployments (Shane expressed skepticism: 'ask them what customers are using their platforms… I'll guarantee 50% of it is not ready'), and (6) best-of-breed capability vs suite trade-off. No formal scoring rubric, weighting, or decision thresholds were shared. Inferred: BT will shortlist vendors from the RFI for an RFP, then evaluate demos, references, and commercial proposals. Recommended move: request the RFI questions and scoring criteria from Shane so Zycus can tailor its capability pack and positioning to BT's stated evaluation framework.

north_star_verdict.headline: Zycus missed the orchestration and AI sourcing RFI windows (closed over a month ago as of 30 June 2026). BT is now evaluating competitor submissions (GEP, Zip, Globality). An RFP is expected but timing is unknown. The deal is genuinely dark: the single buyer touchpoint occurred on 30 June 2026, one day before this sweep, and no follow-up engagement has occurred since. Without an RFP invitation within 2-4 weeks, the deal cannot progress.

flags: No champion secured or developing. Shane (influencer) showed interest but no advocacy evidence in 13+ months. No introduction to EB/DM, no internal intel shared, no driving of agenda. Risk: if Shane has deprioritized or left, deal has no internal sponsor.

No quantified value case. Shane articulated orchestration fragmentation pain and Certio £500k/year cost, but no cycle-time savings, FTE reduction, error-rate improvement, or ROI quantified. Risk: without business case, EB cannot justify investment and RFP shortlisting favors competitors with quantified ROI.

Missed two critical RFI windows (orchestration closed 'next 2 to 3 weeks' from May 2025; AI sourcing also closed). Zycus has no RFI submission, no shortlist confirmation, and unknown RFP invitation status. Risk: excluded from RFP by default unless late addition allowed and secured immediately.
Full decision criteria unknown: Zycus has not obtained the RFI questions or scoring rubric BT used to evaluate competitors.
Zero buyer engagement on record (LastActivityDate null, no Avoma calls). Re-engage Shane immediately and request decision-committee introductions.
best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers for this deal: (1) Overcome production-readiness skepticism by providing 3 named enterprise customers using Zycus orchestration in production with reference contacts. (2) Multi-thread to Simen Wefald (Shane's boss) and the new CPO-equivalent (budget owner) to map decision authority and build executive sponsorship before the RFP shortlist locks. (3) Secure the RFI questions and scoring criteria from Shane to tailor the capability pack to BT's stated evaluation framework.

competitive_position.summary: Globality (incumbent sourcing platform) and GEP (RFI finalist, peer competitor) are the strongest current threats as of 30 June 2026. Globality has incumbent advantage (existing relationship, data integration) and is in play for replacement via the AI sourcing RFI BT issued. GEP is a known RFI participant and Zycus peer in orchestration. Certio (incumbent savings platform) is a medium threat (Shane expressed dissatisfaction: 'is it the perfect tool?'). Zip is a medium threat (RFI participant). Zycus missed both RFI windows and is not in the current evaluation. Competitive wedge: Zycus must prove production-ready orchestration + agentic AI sourcing + savings lifecycle tracking with named enterprise references to overcome Shane's skepticism ('50% of it is not ready') and secure an RFP invitation.

competitors.name: GEP
competitors.quote: I know you'll probably be inviting people like Zip and GEP… we're winning in that space
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus orchestration + AI sourcing + savings as an integrated best-of-breed suite vs GEP's point-solution gaps. Provide production orchestration references and live demo showing customer workflows (not generic marketing). Emphasize Zycus wins against GEP in orchestration as stated by Anthony.

competitors.name: Zip
competitors.how_we_win: Same as GEP: integrated suite positioning, production references, live orchestration demo. Emphasize Zycus production-readiness vs Zip's unproven claims.

competitors.name: Globality (incumbent sourcing platform)
competitors.quote: Shane Gallagher: 'we use Globality for sourcing'
competitors.source: Avoma intro call 30 Jun 2026, Shane Gallagher
competitors.how_we_win: Globality is the current sourcing platform but BT Sourced is evaluating alternatives (orchestration and AI sourcing RFIs issued). Inertia is real but Shane is open to change. Position Zycus as a modular upgrade: keep Globality if needed, add Zycus orchestration (intake/front-door) and savings management (lifecycle tracking with AI insights) as point solutions, then expand to agentic sourcing (Anna) in Phase 2. Show integration capability via open APIs and prove Zycus can co-exist with or replace Globality.

competitors.name: Certio (incumbent savings tracking platform)
competitors.quote: Shane Gallagher: 'we use Certio for savings… is it the perfect tool?'
competitors.source: Avoma intro call 30 Jun 2026, Shane Gallagher
competitors.how_we_win: Certio is under evaluation ('is it the perfect tool?'). Shane wants lifecycle savings tracking with stage-gate delta capture and AI-driven insight generation from historical context — exactly what Zycus savings management + Insight Studio delivers. Position Zycus as the AI-native upgrade to Certio: conversational AI front-end, milestone tracking, obligation management, auditable trail, and AI-surfaced insights from savings project history. Deliver a demo video showing the Insight Studio savings lifecycle dashboard and AI-driven delta capture across stage gates, then offer a live demo comparing Zycus vs Certio on a real BT Sourced savings use case.

competitors.name: Globality
competitors.source: Shane Gallagher, Avoma Zycus <> BTS Intro, 30 June 2026
competitors.how_we_win: Prove Zycus agentic AI sourcing (Anna, autonomous negotiation, bid recommendation) is production-ready and superior to Globality's AI. Provide 3 named enterprise customers using Zycus AI sourcing in production with reference contacts. Position Merlin as the next-gen agentic layer Globality cannot match.

competitors.name: Certio
competitors.quote: We use Certio for saving, tracking… is it the perfect tool?
competitors.how_we_win: Prove Zycus savings management with lifecycle tracking and milestone/delta capture (Shane's stated need) exceeds Certio's capability. Demonstrate AI-driven insights and project-level obligation tracking Certio cannot deliver.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus positioned AI sourcing (Anna, agentic flows, autonomous negotiation) and orchestration (Teams front door, conversational AI) on the 30 June 2026 call. Anthony Gray stated 'we were the first agentic source to pay platform announced two years ago… we have got live real customers using different various of agents' and named Tata and a large GSI as production references. Shane expressed interest in agentic AI sourcing and savings management but requested materials and demo videos for internal review before committing to a live demo. No AIS score on record. AI positioning is aligned with Shane's stated needs (agentic sourcing, AI-driven savings insights), but production-readiness skepticism is a risk.

Zycus positioning aligned on modular approach and AI sourcing, but production-readiness skepticism is a risk.
